title: TaqMan qPCR INRAE eWHALE
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"63esp","text":"Protocol for the TaqMan assay used by INRAE team in eWHALE ring test study","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"b2r45","text":"Lab work:","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6jq01","text":"Put all equipment (micropipettes, disposable tips, microtubes, molecular grade water, qPCR plate) in UV cabinet and\nlight UV for 20 mins.","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"an7he","text":"Thaw, vortex and spin samples.","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5rqds","text":"Thaw and vortex Master Mix environnemental TaqMan‱ 2.0.","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":49,"length":1}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"emoji","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"EDB8184FCC1D11EE973C0A58A9FEAC02","name":":tm:"}}}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"cnchu","text":"- Pipettes : monochannel p20, p100, p200, p1000, multichannel p20 and corresponding filter tips","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dqvlu","text":"- 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tubes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Determine the number of reactions, including negative controls and NTCs. Add 2-3 reactions to have margin for pipetting error. Prepare the Master mix according following this volume for 1 reaction : 4 µL of Molecular Grade Water 10 µL Master Mix environnemental TaqMan‱ 2.0 1 µL Probe at 0.13 micromolar (µM) 1 µL Forward primer at 0.5 micromolar (µM) 1 µL Reverse primer at 0.5 micromolar (µM) Vortex and spin.
Add 17 µL of Master mix. Add 3 µL of Sample . Seal the plate with qPCR plate seal and centrifuge briefly ( 00:02:00 at 2000g).
Put the plate in the thermal cycler. qPCR is performed with an initial denaturation at 95 °C for 00:10:00 followed by 49 cycles of 00:00:30 at 95 °C and 00:00:50 at 60 °C .
creator: Teddy Urvois
